text: That is a great question. Let me think for a second.
In other words, we choose the safer value.
To be honest, the answer depends on the market.
Let me check and get back to you on that.
Marcos, how do you make sure a valuation is fair when the market is so unstable?
I see. Could you explain how exactly?
To put it simply, when prices are unstable, we choose the safer value. In other words, we stay conservative.
And how long does the whole process take, roughly?
Roughly two weeks. I touched on the timeline earlier, but bear with me if you want the exact figure.
No need. One last thing -- what is the exact margin of error?
I do not have that number with me right now. Let me check and get back to you on that.
To put it simply, we find a fair price.
To be honest, it depends on the market.
Roughly two weeks, but bear with me.

Answering tough questions is a skill, and like any skill, you can learn it. Here are my three best tips. First, stay calm and buy yourself a few seconds. A simple opener like, that is a great question, gives your brain time to organize the answer. Second, be honest. If you do not know something, never guess a number. Just say you will get back to the person with the exact figure. Honesty builds trust far more than a wrong answer. And third, when your answer is complex, paraphrase it. Use a phrase like, in other words, or, to put it simply, and then give a short, clear version. Remember: a calm, honest, simple answer is always stronger than a fast, nervous one. Take your time, and you will do great.
That is a great question. Let me think.
In other words, we choose the safer value.
To be honest, it depends on the market.
Let me check and get back to you on that.
Bear with me while I check the figure.
